subtasks.description: Query SQL warehouse to compute current month spend and 3-month rolling average by cost center/department, calculate variance ratio, and identify anomalies
Reconcile current spend anomalies with historical variance patterns, apply classification thresholds (normal_variance, concerning, critical) based on deviation magnitude and volatility signals

plan.cost_rationale: Two subtasks with minimal decomposition: (1) sql_gen at mid-tier to handle multi-CTE aggregation (current month + 3-month rolling average + variance calculation) across likely multiple cost dimensions, requiring window functions and grouping; (2) cross_recon at mid-tier to apply classification logic that interprets variance signals and assigns risk tiers based on business thresholds—this is a single reconciliation step that combines anomaly interpretation with classification, not a separate policy check. No separate policy_action needed because classification is a business decision, not a governance/compliance gate. No additional subtasks because both operations can complete in sequence with the minimal hand-offs shown.

output.raw_output:
{"sql": "WITH monthly_spend AS (
  SELECT
    publisher AS cost_center,
    DATE_TRUNC('month', event_date) AS spend_month,
    SUM(amount_usd) AS total_spend
  FROM consumption_facts
  WHERE event_date >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE) - INTERVAL '4 months'
  GROUP BY publisher, DATE_TRUNC('month', event_date)
),
current_month AS (
  SELECT
    cost_center,
    total_spend AS current_month_spend
  FROM monthly_spend
  WHERE spend_month = DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE)
),
rolling_avg AS (
  SELECT
    cost_center,
    AVG(total_spend) AS avg_3mo_spend,
    STDDEV(total_spend) AS stddev_3mo_spend
  FROM monthly_spend
  WHERE spend_month >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE) - INTERVAL '3 months'
    AND spend_month < DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE)
  GROUP BY cost_center
),
variance_calc AS (
  SELECT
    cm.cost_center,
    cm.current_month_spend,
    COALESCE(ra.avg_3mo_spend, 0) AS avg_3mo_spend,
    COALESCE(ra.stddev_3mo_spend, 0) AS stddev_3mo_spend,
    CASE
      WHEN COALESCE(ra.avg_3mo_spend, 0) = 0 THEN NULL
      ELSE ROUND(((cm.current_month_spend - ra.avg_3mo_spend) / ra.avg_3mo_spend) * 100, 2)
    END AS variance_pct,
    CASE
      WHEN COALESCE(ra.avg_3mo_spend, 0) = 0 OR COALESCE(ra.stddev_3mo_spend, 0) = 0 THEN NULL
      ELSE ROUND((cm.current_month_spend - ra.avg_3mo_spend) / ra.stddev_3mo_spend, 4)
    END AS z_score
  FROM current_month cm
  LEFT JOIN rolling_avg ra ON cm.cost_center = ra.cost_center
)
SELECT
  cost_center,
  ROUND(current_month_spend, 2) AS current_month_spend,
  ROUND(avg_3mo_spend, 2) AS avg_3mo_spend,
  ROUND(stddev_3mo_spend, 2) AS stddev_3mo_spend,
  variance_pct,
  z_score,
  CASE
    WHEN variance_pct IS NULL THEN 'insufficient_data'
    WHEN ABS(variance_pct) <= 15 THEN 'normal_variance'
    WHEN ABS(variance_pct) > 15 AND ABS(variance_pct) <= 50 THEN 'concerning'
    WHEN ABS(variance_pct) > 50 THEN 'critical'
    ELSE 'normal_variance'
  END AS anomaly_classification,
  CASE
    WHEN current_month_spend > avg_3mo
